guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia) — Necrotising skin and soft tissue infections
Surviving Sepsis Campaign / ANZ sepsis pathway
intentional_test_features: Anchoring / premature-closure trap: a minor garden scratch with early, unimpressive-looking skin findings but pain grossly out of proportion — the classic cellulitis mimic that must NOT be under-triaged.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who palpated subcutaneous crepitus, tested for cutaneous anaesthesia, measured vitals and gave IV ceftriaxone — the AI Doctor CANNOT examine, measure vitals without a device, or give IV drugs, and is scored on recognising a limb- and life-threatening deep soft-tissue infection with sepsis and escalating to 000 immediately.
Discriminates necrotising fasciitis from uncomplicated cellulitis, DVT and compartment syndrome on the pain-out-of-proportion + rapid spread + haemorrhagic bullae constellation.
Age and sex not stated in the source note — inferred adult and flagged; no paediatric dosing concern (adult, 85 kg).

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
